__inputs.name: DS_AZURE_MONITOR
__inputs.label: Azure Monitor (fleet)
__inputs.description: Azure Monitor data source on Movate's Managed Grafana, bound to a system-assigned managed identity. In Phase 1 (ADR 039 D2) the data source enumerates the customer Log Analytics workspaces delegated via Azure Lighthouse (Reader + Monitoring Reader, scoped to the workspace). In Phase 2 the same UID points at Movate's central workspace fed by the opt-in OTLP dual-export.

description: ADR-039 illustrative -- Movate fleet adoption + version posture across MDK deployments. Tagged 'illustrative -- pending ADR 039 sign-off; assumes a customer template variable resolves either via Lighthouse subscription scope (Phase 1) or via the customer OTel attribute (Phase 2)'. Built only from instrument names confirmed on origin/main per docs/observability.md + src/movate/tracing/metrics.py:METRIC_NAMES.

panels.description: Prescriptive runbook for this pane. Read this before paging anyone.

options.content:
### Adoption + version posture -- runbook

**What this pane answers:** how many MDK deployments are live across the fleet, how many agents they each run, which CalVer release is on what env, and how far behind the latest customers are. **Audience:** Movate product + SRE; not for customer-tenant import (use `dashboards/grafana/*.json` for that).

**Normal:** active deployments grows monotonically; weekly new-agents is a steady-state non-zero number; upgrade lag for the production tier sits inside a 14-day window from latest CalVer; no env shows >30 days behind latest CalVer.

**If red:** 
* **Upgrade lag > 30d on a customer's prod env** -- not on-call; open a customer-success ticket; cross-reference the release notes the customer is on (CalVer annotation on each panel, ADR 039 Open Question 3).
* **Active deployments drops** -- check the per-customer health pane (`health.json`) first; a delegation expiry (ADR 039 D2 Phase 1) shows as a *missing* deployment, not a sick one.
* **`new-agents/week == 0` for a customer's prod for 2 weeks** -- adoption signal, not an incident; raise in the next product review.

**Cross-refs:** ADR 039 D1 (hosting), ADR 028 (templates -- top-template panel ties to template adoption), ADR 027 (`mdk dev` -- new-agents pipeline). All data from `agent.execute` span + `service.version` resource attribute (CalVer).

panels.description: Count of distinct MDK deployments reporting in the window, grouped by environment. A 'deployment' is a distinct (customer, deployment) tuple resolved from the AM resource scope (Phase 1) or the customer + deployment OTel resource attributes (Phase 2). Derived from any mdk.* metric emission -- mdk.jobs.completed is the most reliable heartbeat.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.jobs.completed'
| where TimeGenerated > ago($__interval)
| extend env = tostring(Properties['environment'])
| where env in ($environment) or '$environment' == 'All'
| summarize Deployments = dcount(Resource) by env
| order by env asc

panels.title: Active deployments by environment

panels.description: Per-deployment count of distinct agent names seen on agent.execute spans in the window. The 'agent' attribute on agent.execute is the span attribute confirmed in docs/observability.md.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
AppDependencies
| where Name == 'agent.execute'
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| extend agent = tostring(Properties['agent'])
| extend cust  = tostring(Properties['customer'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize ActiveAgents = dcount(agent) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d), cust
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Active agents per customer (7d, daily distinct)

panels.description: Count of NEW agent names appearing this week vs prior weeks. Uses the agent attribute on agent.execute -- first appearance per (customer, agent) pair.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
AppDependencies
| where Name == 'agent.execute'
| where TimeGenerated > ago(60d)
| extend agent = tostring(Properties['agent']), cust = tostring(Properties['customer'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated) by cust, agent
| where FirstSeen > ago(7d)
| count

panels.title: New agents this week

panels.description: **Scaffold -- needs new instrument.** Top template adoption requires either a `template` attribute on agent.execute (does not exist today per docs/observability.md) or a control-plane audit signal joined in. See ADR 039 Open Questions + ADR 028 (template discoverability).

options.content:
### Top template (table) -- scaffold

**Needs new instrument:** `template` attribute on the `agent.execute` span, or a control-plane `template_used` counter.

Not emitted today per `docs/observability.md` -- see **ADR 039 Open Questions** + **ADR 028 (template discoverability)**. The panel ships with shape only.

When the instrument lands, the query is:

```kql
AppDependencies
| where Name == 'agent.execute'
| extend tmpl = tostring(Properties['template'])
| where isnotempty(tmpl)
| summarize Runs = count() by tmpl
| top 10 by Runs desc
```

panels.description: Which CalVer is currently running on each (customer, deployment, env). CalVer YYYY.M.D.N comes from the OTel `service.version` resource attribute (Azure Monitor exposes it on the AppMetrics / AppDependencies Resource column; see ADR 020 for the OTel resource pipeline).

azureLogAnalytics.query:
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.jobs.completed'
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']), depl = tostring(Properties['deployment']), env = tostring(Properties['environment']), ver = tostring(Properties['service.version'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| where env  in ($environment) or '$environment' == 'All'
| summarize LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated) by cust, depl, env, ver
| order by cust asc, env asc

panels.title: CalVer running where

panels.description: Upgrade-lag heatmap: for each (customer, env), days behind the latest CalVer in the fleet. Cells turn red as lag exceeds 30 days. Latest-CalVer is computed inline as the max CalVer observed anywhere in the window.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let latest = toscalar(
  AppMetrics
  | where Name == 'mdk.jobs.completed'
  | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
  | extend ver = tostring(Properties['service.version'])
  | summarize max(ver));
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.jobs.completed'
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']), env = tostring(Properties['environment']), ver = tostring(Properties['service.version'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize ver = max(ver) by cust, env
| extend LagDays = datetime_diff('day', now(), todatetime(latest))
| project cust, env, LagDays

panels.title: Upgrade lag (days behind latest CalVer)

list.definition: Phase 1: Lighthouse subscription display names. Phase 2: distinct values of the `customer` OTel resource attribute (hashed per ADR 039 D4).

list.name: customer

list.definition: Environment slug (prod/staging/dev) from the `environment` OTel resource attribute.

title: Movate fleet -- adoption + version posture (illustrative, ADR 039)
